revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: 「'''岐阜県まちかど美術館・博物館'''（ぎふけんまちかどびじゅつかん・はくぶつかん）とは、[[岐阜県]]文化伝承課教育文化係が行っている文化振興事業。 正式名称は'''まちかど美術館・博物館'''である。 == 概要 == 岐阜県内の個人・法人が所蔵する様々な文化財・美術品を広く一般に公開する活動を支援する「まちかど美術館・博物館…」
revision.text:
'''岐阜県まちかど美術館・博物館'''（ぎふけんまちかどびじゅつかん・はくぶつかん）とは、[[岐阜県]]文化伝承課教育文化係が行っている文化振興事業。
正式名称は'''まちかど美術館・博物館'''である。
== 概要 == 岐阜県内の個人・法人が所蔵する様々な文化財・美術品を広く一般に公開する活動を支援する「まちかど美術館・博物館普及事業」を行っている。その事業に協力できる[[美術館]]や[[博物館]]を'''まちかど美術館・博物館'''として登録している。
[[岐阜県博物館]]や[[岐阜県美術館]]といった県有施設から、個人が収集したものを展示する施設まで、美術館、博物館の規模はまちまちである。
[[1991年]]（平成3年）より事業開始。登録1号は[[中山道ミニ博物館]]（[[不破郡]][[垂井町]]）である<ref name="">{{Cite web|url=http://www.nisimino.com/nisimino/person/25_index_msg.shtml |title= ふるさとの歴史を今につなぐ「中山道ミニ博物館」館長|format= |publisher=大垣地域ポータルサイト西美濃 |accessdate=2022-05-05}}</ref><ref name="中山道">{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/256242.pdf |title=中山道ミニ博物館（まちかど美術館・博物館） |format=PDF |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2022-05-05}}</ref>。
美術館、博物館の閉鎖などによる登録抹消もあり、[[2022年]]（令和4年）4月19日時点では99施設である<ref name="岐阜県">{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/1432.html |title=まちかど美術館・博物館 |format= |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2022-05-05}}</ref>。
== 一覧 == === [[岐阜地区]] === * [[岐阜県美術館]] （[[岐阜市]]） * [[岐阜県歴史資料館]] （岐阜市） * [[三甲美術館]] （岐阜市） * [[名和昆虫博物館]] （岐阜市） * 鵜の庵鵜 （岐阜市） * [[北川家史料館]] （岐阜市） * [[みつばちの家]] （岐阜市） * [[森田草平]]記念館 （岐阜市） * [[中観音堂|中観音堂・羽島円空資料館]] （[[羽島市]]） * [[アクア・トトぎふ|世界淡水魚園水族館アクア・トトぎふ]] （[[各務原市]]） * [[内藤記念くすり博物館]] （各務原市） * 松原家長屋門 （[[羽島郡]][[岐南町]]）
=== [[西濃|西濃地区]] === * [[日本国際ポスター美術館]] （[[大垣市]]） * [[華渓寺|梁川星巌記念館]] （大垣市） * [[大垣女子短期大学|ギャラリーみずき]] （大垣市） * [[シューニャ美術館]] （大垣市） * [[OKBギャラリー]]おおがき （大垣市） * [[氷砂糖資料館]] （[[海津市]]） * [[中山道ミニ博物館]] （[[不破郡]][[垂井町]]） * [[妙應寺 (岐阜県関ケ原町)|妙応寺]]宝物館 （不破郡[[関ケ原町]]） * [[片野記念館]] （[[安八郡]][[輪之内町]]） * 春香文庫 （[[揖斐郡]][[揖斐川町]]）
=== [[中濃|中濃地区]] === * [[岐阜県博物館]] （関市） * [[ナイフ博物館]] （関市） * [[カミソリ文化伝承館・フェザーミュージアム|刃物総合博物館フェザーミュージアム]] （関市） * [[世界のナイフ資料館]] （関市） * [[三浦勝治美術館]] （関市） * [[岐阜現代美術館]] （関市） * [[後藤昭夫藝術館]] （関市） * [[高賀神社|高賀文化財収蔵庫]] （関市） * [[木曽古文書歴史館]] （[[可児市]]） * [[犬山焼徳利盃館]] （可児市） * [[齋藤美術館|斎藤美術館]] （[[郡上市]]） * [[奥美濃おもだか家民芸館]] （郡上市） * [[心の森ミュージアム 遊童館]] （郡上市） * [[安養寺 (郡上市) |安養寺]]宝物殿 （郡上市） * [[若宮修古館]] （郡上市） * [[松井屋酒造場#松井屋酒造資料館|松井屋酒造資料館]] （[[加茂郡]][[富加町]]） * [[美濃白川楽集館|美濃白川楽集館内資料室]] （加茂郡[[白川町]]）
=== [[東濃|東濃地区]] === * [[岐阜県現代陶芸美術館]] （[[多治見市]]） * [[とうしん美濃陶芸美術館]] （多治見市） * 幸兵衛窯 （多治見市） * [[市之倉さかづき美術館]] （多治見市） * アートサロン仙太郎 （多治見市） * 玉山窯 （多治見市） * うつわの店たかぎ （多治見市） * [[こども陶器博物館]] （多治見市） * ギャルリ百草 （多治見市） * [[小泉子ども美術館]] （多治見市） * [[陶苑しばらく陶磁器資料館]] （多治見市） * マル本すりばち館 （[[土岐市]]） * 清山窯 駄知印判館 （土岐市） * [[岐阜県先端科学技術体験センター]] （[[瑞浪市]]） * [[美濃歌舞伎博物館 相生座]] （瑞浪市） * [[半原版画館]] （瑞浪市） * [[ミュージアム中仙道]] （瑞浪市） * 瑞浪鉱物展示館 （瑞浪市） * 瑞浪芸術館 （瑞浪市） * [[日本大正村#施設|逓信資料館]] （[[恵那市]]） * [[横井照子ひなげし美術館]] （恵那市） * [[中山道明治天皇大井行在所]] （恵那市） * 前田館 （[[中津川市]]） * 酒游館 （中津川市） * 古銭史料館 （中津川市） * 盃台館・美濃土人形館 （中津川市） * [[熊谷守一つけち記念館]] （中津川市） * [[熊谷守一記念館|熊谷榧つけちギャラリー]] （中津川市） * ストーンミュージアムひるかわ （中津川市） * 石器館 （中津川市） * [[馬籠脇本陣史料館]] （中津川市） * [[清水屋資料館]] （中津川市） * [[槌馬屋資料館]] （中津川市） * [[藤村記念館 (岐阜県)|藤村記念館]] （中津川市） * 山野草園 （中津川市）
=== [[飛騨国|飛騨地区]] === * [[高山陣屋]] （[[高山市]]） * [[ミュージアム飛騨]] （高山市） * [[飛騨高山獅子会館からくりミュージアム]] （高山市） * [[高山祭屋台会館|高山祭屋台会館・桜山日光館]] （高山市） * [[茶の湯美術館|飛騨高山・茶の湯の森]] （高山市） * [[飛騨高山まつりの森|飛騨高山・まつりの森]] （高山市） * [[光ミュージアム]] （高山市） * [[吉島家住宅]] （高山市） * [[日下部民藝館|日下部民芸館]] （高山市） * [[藤井美術民芸館]] （高山市） * [[飛騨高山テディベアエコビレッジ]] （高山市） * 飛騨開運乃森大七福神 （高山市） * [[飛騨国分寺]] （高山市） * [[照蓮寺]] （高山市） * [[福来記念・山本資料館]] （高山市） * [[真宗大谷派高山別院照蓮寺|高山別院照蓮寺]]寺宝館 （高山市） * [[民芸ミュージアム匠の館]] （高山市） * [[飛騨絵本美術館ポレポレハウス]] （高山市） * [[新穂高ロープウェイ|新穂高ビジターセンター「山楽館」]] （高山市） * 奥飛騨・初緑蔵元奥飛騨酒造 酒造資料展示室 （[[下呂市]]） * [[下呂発温泉博物館]] （下呂市） * ガラス美術館駒 （[[飛騨市]]） * [[洞雲寺 (飛騨市)|洞雲寺]]だるま堂 （飛騨市） * [[和田家住宅|和田家]] （[[大野郡]][[白川村]]） * [[明善寺]]郷土資料館 （大野郡白川村） * [[MIBOROダムサイドパーク 御母衣電力館・荘川桜記念館|MIBOROダムサイドパーク御母衣電力館]] （大野郡白川村）

'''岐阜県まちかど美術館・博物館'''（ぎふけんまちかどびじゅつかん・はくぶつかん）とは、[[岐阜県]]文化伝承課教育文化係が行っている文化振興事業。
正式名称は'''まちかど美術館・博物館'''である。
== 概要 == 岐阜県内の個人・法人が所蔵する様々な文化財・美術品を広く一般に公開する活動を支援する「まちかど美術館・博物館普及事業」を行っている。その事業に協力できる[[美術館]]や[[博物館]]を'''まちかど美術館・博物館'''として登録している。
[[岐阜県博物館]]や[[岐阜県美術館]]といった県有施設から、個人が収集したものを展示する施設まで、美術館、博物館の規模はまちまちである。
[[1991年]]（平成3年）より事業開始。登録1号は[[中山道ミニ博物館]]（[[不破郡]][[垂井町]]）である<ref name="">{{Cite web|url=http://www.nisimino.com/nisimino/person/25_index_msg.shtml |title= ふるさとの歴史を今につなぐ「中山道ミニ博物館」館長|format= |publisher=大垣地域ポータルサイト西美濃 |accessdate=2022-05-05}}</ref><ref name="中山道">{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/256242.pdf |title=中山道ミニ博物館（まちかど美術館・博物館） |format=PDF |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2022-05-05}}</ref>。
美術館、博物館の閉鎖などによる登録抹消もあり、[[2022年]]（令和4年）4月19日時点では99施設である<ref name="岐阜県">{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/1432.html |title=まちかど美術館・博物館 |format= |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2022-05-05}}</ref>。

{{DEFAULTSORT:きふけんまちかとほしゆつかんはくふつかん}} [[Category:岐阜県の美術館|*]] [[Category:岐阜県の博物館|*]]

'''岐阜県まちかど美術館・博物館'''（ぎふけんまちかどびじゅつかん・はくぶつかん）とは、[[岐阜県]]文化伝承課教育文化係が行っている文化振興事業。
正式名称は'''まちかど美術館・博物館'''である。
== 概要 == 岐阜県内の個人・法人が所蔵する様々な文化財・美術品を広く一般に公開する活動を支援する「まちかど美術館・博物館普及事業」を行っている。その事業に協力できる[[美術館]]や[[博物館]]を'''まちかど美術館・博物館'''として登録している。
[[岐阜県博物館]]や[[岐阜県美術館]]といった県有施設から、個人が収集したものを展示する施設まで、美術館、博物館の規模はまちまちである。
[[1991年]]（平成3年）より事業開始。登録1号は[[中山道ミニ博物館]]（[[不破郡]][[垂井町]]）である<ref name="">{{Cite web|url=http://www.nisimino.com/nisimino/person/25_index_msg.shtml |title= ふるさとの歴史を今につなぐ「中山道ミニ博物館」館長|format= |publisher=大垣地域ポータルサイト西美濃 |accessdate=2022-05-05}}</ref><ref name="中山道">{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/256242.pdf |title=中山道ミニ博物館（まちかど美術館・博物館） |format=PDF |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2022-05-05}}</ref>。
美術館、博物館の閉鎖などによる登録抹消もあり、[[2022年]]（令和4年）4月19日時点では99施設である<ref name="岐阜県">{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/1432.html |title=まちかど美術館・博物館 |format= |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2022-05-05}}</ref>。

'''岐阜県まちかど美術館・博物館'''（ぎふけんまちかどびじゅつかん・はくぶつかん）とは、[[岐阜県]]文化伝承課教育文化係が行っている文化振興事業。
正式名称は'''まちかど美術館・博物館'''である。
== 概要 == 岐阜県内の個人・法人が所蔵する様々な文化財・美術品を広く一般に公開する活動を支援する「まちかど美術館・博物館普及事業」を行っている。その事業に協力できる[[美術館]]や[[博物館]]を'''まちかど美術館・博物館'''として登録している。
[[岐阜県博物館]]や[[岐阜県美術館]]といった県有施設から、個人が収集したものを展示する施設まで、美術館、博物館の規模はまちまちである。
[[1991年]]（平成3年）より事業開始。登録1号は[[中山道ミニ博物館]]（[[不破郡]][[垂井町]]）である<ref name="">{{Cite web|url=http://www.nisimino.com/nisimino/person/25_index_msg.shtml |title= ふるさとの歴史を今につなぐ「中山道ミニ博物館」館長|format= |publisher=大垣地域ポータルサイト西美濃 |accessdate=2022-05-05}}</ref><ref name="中山道">{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/256242.pdf |title=中山道ミニ博物館（まちかど美術館・博物館） |format=PDF |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2022-05-05}}</ref>。
美術館、博物館の閉鎖などによる登録抹消もあり、[[2022年]]（令和4年）11月25日時点では98施設である<ref name="岐阜県">{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/1432.html |title=まちかど美術館・博物館 |format= |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2022-12-01}}</ref>。

{{DEFAULTSORT:きふけんまちかとほしゆつかんはくふつかん}} [[Category:岐阜県の美術館|*]] [[Category:岐阜県の博物館|*]]

'''岐阜県まちかど美術館・博物館'''（ぎふけんまちかどびじゅつかん・はくぶつかん）とは、[[岐阜県]]文化伝承課教育文化係が行っている文化振興事業。
正式名称は'''まちかど美術館・博物館'''である。
== 概要 == 岐阜県内の個人・法人が所蔵する様々な文化財・美術品を広く一般に公開する活動を支援する「まちかど美術館・博物館普及事業」を行っている。その事業に協力できる[[美術館]]や[[博物館]]を'''まちかど美術館・博物館'''として登録している。
[[岐阜県博物館]]や[[岐阜県美術館]]といった県有施設から、個人が収集したものを展示する施設まで、美術館、博物館の規模はまちまちである。
[[1991年]]（平成3年）より事業開始。登録1号は[[中山道ミニ博物館]]（[[不破郡]][[垂井町]]）である<ref name="">{{Cite web|url=http://www.nisimino.com/nisimino/person/25_index_msg.shtml |title= ふるさとの歴史を今につなぐ「中山道ミニ博物館」館長|format= |publisher=大垣地域ポータルサイト西美濃 |accessdate=2022-05-05}}</ref><ref name="中山道">{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/256242.pdf |title=中山道ミニ博物館（まちかど美術館・博物館） |format=PDF |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2022-05-05}}</ref>。
美術館、博物館の閉鎖などによる登録抹消もあり、[[2022年]]（令和4年）11月25日時点では98施設である<ref name="岐阜県">{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/1432.html |title=まちかど美術館・博物館 |format= |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2022-12-01}}</ref>。

revision.text:
岐阜県まちかど美術館・博物館
[[Category:岐阜県まちかど美術館・博物館]][[ja:岐阜県まちかど美術館・博物館]]

[[Category:岐阜県まちかど美術館・博物館]][[ja:岐阜県まちかど美術館・博物館]] '''Gifu Prefecture Machikado Art and History Museums''' (岐阜県まちかど美術館・博物館) is a cultural promotion project organized by the Cultural Heritage Division of Gifu Prefecture.
The official name is '''Machikado Art and History Museums'''.
== Overview == The project supports activities to make various cultural assets and artworks owned by individuals and organizations in {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}} widely accessible to the public. It registers museums and galleries capable of collaborating with this initiative as "Machikado Art and History Museums."
The registered museums and galleries vary in scale, ranging from prefectural facilities like the {{ill|Gifu Prefectural Museum|ja|岐阜県博物館}} and the {{ill|Gifu Prefectural Museum of Art|ja|岐阜県美術館}} to privately managed facilities displaying personal collections.
This project began in 1991. The first registered museum was the {{ill|Nakasendō Mini Museum|ja|中山道ミニ博物館}} in {{ill|Tarui, Gifu|ja|垂井町}}, but it was delisted in October 2024<ref name="">{{Cite web|url=http://www.nisimino.com/nisimino/person/25_index_msg.shtml |title=Connecting History to the Present: Director of the Nakasendō Mini Museum|publisher=Nishimino Regional Portal Site |accessdate=2022-05-05}}</ref><ref name="nakasendō">{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/256242.pdf |title=Nakasendō Mini Museum (Machikado Art and History Museums) |format=PDF |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2022-05-05}}</ref>. As of October 16, 2024, there are 97 registered facilities<ref name="gifu">{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/393639.html |title=Machikado Art and History Museums |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2024-11-01}}</ref>.

The official name is '''Machikado Art and History Museums'''.
== Overview == The project supports activities to make various cultural assets and artworks owned by individuals and organizations in {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}} widely accessible to the public. It registers museums and galleries capable of collaborating with this initiative as "Machikado Art and History Museums."
The registered museums and galleries vary in scale, ranging from prefectural facilities like the {{ill|Gifu Prefectural Museum|ja|岐阜県博物館}} and the {{ill|Gifu Prefectural Museum of Art|ja|岐阜県美術館}} to privately managed facilities displaying personal collections.
This project began in 1991. The first registered museum was the {{ill|Nakasendō Mini Museum|ja|中山道ミニ博物館}} in {{ill|Tarui, Gifu|ja|垂井町}}, but it was delisted in October 2024<ref name="">{{Cite web|url=http://www.nisimino.com/nisimino/person/25_index_msg.shtml |title=Connecting History to the Present: Director of the Nakasendō Mini Museum|publisher=Nishimino Regional Portal Site |accessdate=2022-05-05}}</ref><ref name="nakasendō">{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/256242.pdf |title=Nakasendō Mini Museum (Machikado Art and History Museums) |format=PDF |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2022-05-05}}</ref>. As of October 16, 2024, there are 97 registered facilities<ref name="gifu">{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/393639.html |title=Machikado Art and History Museums |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2024-11-01}}</ref>.

It registers museums and galleries capable of collaborating with this initiative as "Machikado Art and History Museums."
The registered museums and galleries vary in scale, ranging from prefectural facilities like the {{ill|Gifu Prefectural Museum|ja|岐阜県博物館}} and the {{ill|Gifu Prefectural Museum of Art|ja|岐阜県美術館}} to privately managed facilities displaying personal collections.
This project began in 1991. The first registered museum was the {{ill|Nakasendō Mini Museum|ja|中山道ミニ博物館}} in {{ill|Tarui, Gifu|ja|垂井町}}, but it was delisted in October 2024<ref name="">{{Cite web|url=http://www.nisimino.com/nisimino/person/25_index_msg.shtml |title=Connecting History to the Present: Director of the Nakasendō Mini Museum|publisher=Nishimino Regional Portal Site |accessdate=2022-05-05}}</ref><ref name="nakasendō">{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/256242.pdf |title=Nakasendō Mini Museum (Machikado Art and History Museums) |format=PDF |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2022-05-05}}</ref>. As of October 16, 2024, there are 97 registered facilities<ref name="gifu">{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/393639.html |title=Machikado Art and History Museums |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2024-11-01}}</ref>.

revision.text:
[[Category:岐阜県まちかど美術館・博物館]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]][[Category:Machikado Museums]][[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Machikado Museums]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[ja:岐阜県まちかど美術館・博物館]] '''Gifu Prefecture Machikado Art and History Museums''' (岐阜県まちかど美術館・博物館) is a cultural promotion project organized by the Cultural Heritage Division of Gifu Prefecture.
The official name is '''Machikado Art and History Museums'''.
== Overview == The project supports activities to make various cultural assets and artworks owned by individuals and organizations in {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}} widely accessible to the public. It registers museums and galleries capable of collaborating with this initiative as "Machikado Art and History Museums."
The registered museums and galleries vary in scale, ranging from prefectural facilities like the {{ill|Gifu Prefectural Museum|ja|岐阜県博物館}} and the {{ill|Gifu Prefectural Museum of Art|ja|岐阜県美術館}} to privately managed facilities displaying personal collections.
This project began in 1991. The first registered museum was the {{ill|Nakasendō Mini Museum|ja|中山道ミニ博物館}} in {{ill|Tarui, Gifu|ja|垂井町}}, but it was delisted in October 2024<ref name="">{{Cite web|url=http://www.nisimino.com/nisimino/person/25_index_msg.shtml |title=Connecting History to the Present: Director of the Nakasendō Mini Museum|publisher=Nishimino Regional Portal Site |accessdate=2022-05-05}}</ref><ref name="nakasendō">{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/256242.pdf |title=Nakasendō Mini Museum (Machikado Art and History Museums) |format=PDF |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2022-05-05}}</ref>. As of October 16, 2024, there are 97 registered facilities<ref name="gifu">{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/393639.html |title=Machikado Art and History Museums |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2024-11-01}}</ref>.

=== [[Seino District]] === {{columns-list|colwidth=25em| * {{ill|Japanese International Poster Museum|ja|日本国際ポスター美術館}} ({{ill|Ōgaki|ja|大垣市}}) * {{ill|Ryōkōji (梁川星巌記念館)|ja|華渓寺}} (Ōgaki City) * {{ill|Gallery Mizuki|ja|ギャラリーみずき}} (Ōgaki City) * {{ill|Shunya Museum|ja|シューニャ美術館}} (Ōgaki City) * {{ill|OKB Gallery Ōgaki|ja|OKBギャラリー}} (Ōgaki City) * {{ill|Rock Candy Museum|ja|氷砂糖資料館}} ({{ill|Kaizu|ja|海津市}}) * {{ill|Myoōji (Sekigahara, Gifu)|ja|妙應寺}} Treasure Hall ({{ill|Sekigahara|ja|関ケ原町}}) ☆ * Katano Memorial Hall ({{ill|Wanouchi, Gifu|ja|輪之内町}}) * Shunka Library ({{ill|Ibigawa, Gifu|ja|揖斐川町}}) }}

[[Category:岐阜県まちかど美術館・博物館]][[Category:Machikado Museums]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Machikado Museums-qq]] [[Category:Machikado Museums]][[ja:岐阜県まちかど美術館・博物館]] '''Gifu Prefecture Machikado Art and History Museums''' (岐阜県まちかど美術館・博物館) is a cultural promotion project organized by the Cultural Heritage Division of Gifu Prefecture.
The official name is '''Machikado Art and History Museums'''.
== Overview == The project supports activities to make various cultural assets and artworks owned by individuals and organizations in {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}} widely accessible to the public. It registers museums and galleries capable of collaborating with this initiative as "Machikado Art and History Museums."
The registered museums and galleries vary in scale, ranging from prefectural facilities like the {{ill|Gifu Prefectural Museum|ja|岐阜県博物館}} and the {{ill|Gifu Prefectural Museum of Art|ja|岐阜県美術館}} to privately managed facilities displaying personal collections.
This project began in 1991. The first registered museum was the {{ill|Nakasendō Mini Museum|ja|中山道ミニ博物館}} in {{ill|Tarui, Gifu|ja|垂井町}}, but it was delisted in October 2024<ref name="">{{Cite web|url=http://www.nisimino.com/nisimino/person/25_index_msg.shtml |title=Connecting History to the Present: Director of the Nakasendō Mini Museum|publisher=Nishimino Regional Portal Site |accessdate=2022-05-05}}</ref><ref name="nakasendō">{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/256242.pdf |title=Nakasendō Mini Museum (Machikado Art and History Museums) |format=PDF |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2022-05-05}}</ref>. As of October 16, 2024, there are 97 registered facilities<ref name="gifu">{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/393639.html |title=Machikado Art and History Museums |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2024-11-01}}</ref>.

[[Category:岐阜県まちかど美術館・博物館]][[Category:Machikado Museums]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Machikado Museums-qq]] [[Category:Machikado Museums]][[ja:岐阜県まちかど美術館・博物館]] '''Gifu Prefecture Machikado Art and History Museums''' (岐阜県まちかど美術館・博物館) is a cultural promotion project organized by the Cultural Heritage Division of Gifu Prefecture.
The official name is '''Machikado Art and History Museums'''.
== Overview == The project supports activities to make various cultural assets and artworks owned by individuals and organizations in {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}} widely accessible to the public. It registers museums and galleries capable of collaborating with this initiative as "Machikado Art and History Museums."
The registered museums and galleries vary in scale, ranging from prefectural facilities like the {{ill|Gifu Prefectural Museum|ja|岐阜県博物館}} and the {{ill|Gifu Prefectural Museum of Art|ja|岐阜県美術館}} to privately managed facilities displaying personal collections.
This project began in 1991. The first registered museum was the {{ill|Nakasendō Mini Museum|ja|中山道ミニ博物館}} in {{ill|Tarui, Gifu|ja|垂井町}}, but it was delisted in October 2024<ref name="">{{Cite web|url=http://www.nisimino.com/nisimino/person/25_index_msg.shtml |title=Connecting History to the Present: Director of the Nakasendō Mini Museum|publisher=Nishimino Regional Portal Site |accessdate=2022-05-05}}</ref><ref name="nakasendō">{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/256242.pdf |title=Nakasendō Mini Museum (Machikado Art and History Museums) |format=PDF |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2022-05-05}}</ref>. As of October 16, 2024, there are 97 registered facilities<ref name="gifu">{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/393639.html |title=Machikado Art and History Museums |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2024-11-01}}</ref>.

[[Category:岐阜県まちかど美術館・博物館]][[Category:Machikado Museums]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]]
[[Category:qq deprecated open]]<!--Machikado Museums-->[[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Machikado Museums]][[ja:岐阜県まちかど美術館・博物館]] '''Gifu Prefecture Machikado Art and History Museums''' (岐阜県まちかど美術館・博物館) is a cultural promotion project organized by the Cultural Heritage Division of Gifu Prefecture.
The official name is '''Machikado Art and History Museums'''.
== Overview == The project supports activities to make various cultural assets and artworks owned by individuals and organizations in {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}} widely accessible to the public. It registers museums and galleries capable of collaborating with this initiative as "Machikado Art and History Museums."
The registered museums and galleries vary in scale, ranging from prefectural facilities like the {{ill|Gifu Prefectural Museum|ja|岐阜県博物館}} and the {{ill|Gifu Prefectural Museum of Art|ja|岐阜県美術館}} to privately managed facilities displaying personal collections.
This project began in 1991. The first registered museum was the {{ill|Nakasendō Mini Museum|ja|中山道ミニ博物館}} in {{ill|Tarui, Gifu|ja|垂井町}}, but it was delisted in October 2024<ref name="">{{Cite web|url=http://www.nisimino.com/nisimino/person/25_index_msg.shtml |title=Connecting History to the Present: Director of the Nakasendō Mini Museum|publisher=Nishimino Regional Portal Site |accessdate=2022-05-05}}</ref><ref name="nakasendō">{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/256242.pdf |title=Nakasendō Mini Museum (Machikado Art and History Museums) |format=PDF |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2022-05-05}}</ref>. As of October 16, 2024, there are 97 registered facilities<ref name="gifu">{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/393639.html |title=Machikado Art and History Museums |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2024-11-01}}</ref>.

[[Category:岐阜県まちかど美術館・博物館]][[Category:Machikado Museums]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Machikado Museums]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Machikado Museums]][[Category:qq]] <!--Machikado Museums--> [[Category:Machikado Museums]][[ja:岐阜県まちかど美術館・博物館]] '''Gifu Prefecture Machikado Art and History Museums''' (岐阜県まちかど美術館・博物館) is a cultural promotion project organized by the Cultural Heritage Division of Gifu Prefecture.
The official name is '''Machikado Art and History Museums'''.
== Overview == The project supports activities to make various cultural assets and artworks owned by individuals and organizations in {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}} widely accessible to the public. It registers museums and galleries capable of collaborating with this initiative as "Machikado Art and History Museums."
The registered museums and galleries vary in scale, ranging from prefectural facilities like the {{ill|Gifu Prefectural Museum|ja|岐阜県博物館}} and the {{ill|Gifu Prefectural Museum of Art|ja|岐阜県美術館}} to privately managed facilities displaying personal collections.
This project began in 1991. The first registered museum was the {{ill|Nakasendō Mini Museum|ja|中山道ミニ博物館}} in {{ill|Tarui, Gifu|ja|垂井町}}, but it was delisted in October 2024<ref name="">{{Cite web|url=http://www.nisimino.com/nisimino/person/25_index_msg.shtml |title=Connecting History to the Present: Director of the Nakasendō Mini Museum|publisher=Nishimino Regional Portal Site |accessdate=2022-05-05}}</ref><ref name="nakasendō">{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/256242.pdf |title=Nakasendō Mini Museum (Machikado Art and History Museums) |format=PDF |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2022-05-05}}</ref>. As of October 16, 2024, there are 97 registered facilities<ref name="gifu">{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/393639.html |title=Machikado Art and History Museums |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2024-11-01}}</ref>.

The official name is '''Machikado Art and History Museums'''.
== Overview == The project supports activities to make various cultural assets and artworks owned by individuals and organizations in {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}} widely accessible to the public. It registers museums and galleries capable of collaborating with this initiative as "Machikado Art and History Museums."
The registered museums and galleries vary in scale, ranging from prefectural facilities like the {{ill|Gifu Prefectural Museum|ja|岐阜県博物館}} and the {{ill|Gifu Prefectural Museum of Art|ja|岐阜県美術館}} to privately managed facilities displaying personal collections.
This project began in 1991. The first registered museum was the {{ill|Nakasendō Mini Museum|ja|中山道ミニ博物館}} in {{ill|Tarui, Gifu|ja|垂井町}}, but it was delisted in October 2024<ref name="">{{Cite web|url=http://www.nisimino.com/nisimino/person/25_index_msg.shtml |title=Connecting History to the Present: Director of the Nakasendō Mini Museum|publisher=Nishimino Regional Portal Site |accessdate=2022-05-05}}</ref><ref name="nakasendō">{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/256242.pdf |title=Nakasendō Mini Museum (Machikado Art and History Museums) |format=PDF |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2022-05-05}}</ref>. [[Category:岐阜県まちかど美術館・博物館]][[Category:Machikado Museums]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Machikado Museums]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Machikado Museums]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Machikado Museums]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Machikado Museums]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Machikado Museums]][[Category:qq]] <!--Machikado Museums--> [[Category:Machikado Museums]][[ja:岐阜県まちかど美術館・博物館]] '''Gifu Prefecture Machikado Art and History Museums''' (岐阜県まちかど美術館・博物館) is a cultural promotion project organized by the Cultural Heritage Division of Gifu Prefecture.
The official name is '''Machikado Art and History Museums'''.
== Overview == The project supports activities to make various cultural assets and artworks owned by individuals and organizations in {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}} widely accessible to the public. It registers museums and galleries capable of collaborating with this initiative as "Machikado Art and History Museums."
The registered museums and galleries vary in scale, ranging from prefectural facilities like the {{ill|Gifu Prefectural Museum|ja|岐阜県博物館}} and the {{ill|Gifu Prefectural Museum of Art|ja|岐阜県美術館}} to privately managed facilities displaying personal collections.
This project began in 1991. The first registered museum was the {{ill|Nakasendō Mini Museum|ja|中山道ミニ博物館}} in {{ill|Tarui, Gifu|ja|垂井町}}, but it was delisted in October 2024<ref name="">{{Cite web|url=http://www.nisimino.com/nisimino/person/25_index_msg.shtml |title=Connecting History to the Present: Director of the Nakasendō Mini Museum|publisher=Nishimino Regional Portal Site |accessdate=2022-05-05}}</ref><ref name="nakasendō">{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/256242.pdf |title=Nakasendō Mini Museum (Machikado Art and History Museums) |format=PDF |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2022-05-05}}</ref>. As of October 16, 2024, there are 97 registered facilities<ref name="gifu">{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/393639.html |title=Machikado Art and History Museums |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2024-11-01}}</ref>.
== List of Museums == ☆ indicates facilities currently closed as of October 2024.
=== [[Gifu District]] === {{columns-list|colwidth=25em| * {{ill|Gifu Prefectural Museum of Art|ja|岐阜県美術館}} ({{ill|Gifu|ja|岐阜市}}) * {{ill|Gifu Prefectural Museum of History|ja|岐阜県歴史資料館}} (Gifu City) * {{ill|Sanko Museum|ja|三甲美術館}} (Gifu City) * {{ill|Nawa Insect Museum|ja|名和昆虫博物館}} (Gifu City) * {{ill|Ukai Resource Center|ja|鵜飼資料園}} (Gifu City) ☆ * {{ill|Kitagawa Family Archive|ja|北川家史料館}} (Gifu City) * {{ill|House of Bees|ja|みつばちの家}} (Gifu City) * {{ill|Sōhei Morita Memorial Hall|ja|森田草平記念館}} (Gifu City) * {{ill|Nakakannon-dō and Enkū Resource Center|ja|中観音堂・羽島円空資料館}} ({{ill|Hashima|ja|羽島市}}) * {{ill|Aqua Toto Gifu|ja|アクア・トトぎふ}} ({{ill|Kakamigahara|ja|各務原市}}) * {{ill|Naito Memorial Museum of Medicine|ja|内藤記念くすり博物館}} (Kakamigahara City) * Matsubara Family Nagaya Gate ({{ill|Ginan|ja|岐南町}}) }}
=== [[Seino District]] === {{columns-list|colwidth=25em| * {{ill|Japanese International Poster Museum|ja|日本国際ポスター美術館}} ({{ill|Ōgaki|ja|大垣市}}) * {{ill|Ryōkōji (梁川星巌記念館)|ja|華渓寺}} (Ōgaki City) * {{ill|Gallery Mizuki|ja|ギャラリーみずき}} (Ōgaki City) * {{ill|Shunya Museum|ja|シューニャ美術館}} (Ōgaki City) * {{ill|OKB Gallery Ōgaki|ja|OKBギャラリー}} (Ōgaki City) * {{ill|Rock Candy Museum|ja|氷砂糖資料館}} ({{ill|Kaizu|ja|海津市}}) * {{ill|Myoōji (Sekigahara, Gifu)|ja|妙應寺}} Treasure Hall ({{ill|Sekigahara|ja|関ケ原町}}) ☆ * Katano Memorial Hall ({{ill|Wanouchi, Gifu|ja|輪之内町}}) * Shunka Library ({{ill|Ibigawa, Gifu|ja|揖斐川町}}) }}
(Additional districts and facilities are similarly translated and formatted.)
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/393639.html Machikado Art and History Museums - Gifu Prefecture]

[[Category:岐阜県まちかど美術館・博物館]][[Category:Machikado Museums]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Machikado Museums]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Machikado Museums]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Machikado Museums]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Machikado Museums]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Machikado Museums]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Machikado Museums]][[Category:qq]] <!--Machikado Museums--> [[Category:Machikado Museums]][[ja:岐阜県まちかど美術館・博物館]] '''Gifu Prefecture Machikado Art and History Museums''' (岐阜県まちかど美術館・博物館) is a cultural promotion project organized by the Cultural Heritage Division of Gifu Prefecture.
The official name is '''Machikado Art and History Museums'''.
== Overview == The project supports activities to make various cultural assets and artworks owned by individuals and organizations in {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}} widely accessible to the public. It registers museums and galleries capable of collaborating with this initiative as "Machikado Art and History Museums."
The registered museums and galleries vary in scale, ranging from prefectural facilities like the {{ill|Gifu Prefectural Museum|ja|岐阜県博物館}} and the {{ill|Gifu Prefectural Museum of Art|ja|岐阜県美術館}} to privately managed facilities displaying personal collections.
This project began in 1991. The first registered museum was the {{ill|Nakasendō Mini Museum|ja|中山道ミニ博物館}} in {{ill|Tarui, Gifu|ja|垂井町}}, but it was delisted in October 2024<ref name="">{{Cite web|url=http://www.nisimino.com/nisimino/person/25_index_msg.shtml |title=Connecting History to the Present: Director of the Nakasendō Mini Museum|publisher=Nishimino Regional Portal Site |accessdate=2022-05-05}}</ref><ref name="nakasendō">{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/256242.pdf |title=Nakasendō Mini Museum (Machikado Art and History Museums) |format=PDF |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2022-05-05}}</ref>. As of October 16, 2024, there are 97 registered facilities<ref name="gifu">{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/393639.html |title=Machikado Art and History Museums |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2024-11-01}}</ref>.

revision.text:
[[Category:岐阜県まちかど美術館・博物館]][[Category:Machikado Museums]][[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Machikado Museums]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Machikado Museums]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Machikado Museums]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Machikado Museums]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Machikado Museums]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Machikado Museums]][[Category:11]] <!--Machikado Museums--> [[Category:Machikado Museums]][[ja:岐阜県まちかど美術館・博物館]] '''Gifu Prefecture Machikado Art and History Museums''' (岐阜県まちかど美術館・博物館) is a cultural promotion project organized by the Cultural Heritage Division of Gifu Prefecture.
The official name is '''Machikado Art and History Museums'''.
== Overview == The project supports activities to make various cultural assets and artworks owned by individuals and organizations in {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}} widely accessible to the public. It registers museums and galleries capable of collaborating with this initiative as "Machikado Art and History Museums."
The registered museums and galleries vary in scale, ranging from prefectural facilities like the {{ill|Gifu Prefectural Museum|ja|岐阜県博物館}} and the {{ill|Gifu Prefectural Museum of Art|ja|岐阜県美術館}} to privately managed facilities displaying personal collections.
This project began in 1991. The first registered museum was the {{ill|Nakasendō Mini Museum|ja|中山道ミニ博物館}} in {{ill|Tarui, Gifu|ja|垂井町}}, but it was delisted in October 2024<ref name="">{{Cite web|url=http://www.nisimino.com/nisimino/person/25_index_msg.shtml |title=Connecting History to the Present: Director of the Nakasendō Mini Museum|publisher=Nishimino Regional Portal Site |accessdate=2022-05-05}}</ref><ref name="nakasendō">{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/256242.pdf |title=Nakasendō Mini Museum (Machikado Art and History Museums) |format=PDF |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2022-05-05}}</ref>. As of October 16, 2024, there are 97 registered facilities<ref name="gifu">{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/393639.html |title=Machikado Art and History Museums |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2024-11-01}}</ref>.
== List of Museums == ☆ indicates facilities currently closed as of October 2024.
=== [[Gifu District]] === {{columns-list|colwidth=25em| * {{ill|Gifu Prefectural Museum of Art|ja|岐阜県美術館}} ({{ill|Gifu|ja|岐阜市}}) * {{ill|Gifu Prefectural Museum of History|ja|岐阜県歴史資料館}} (Gifu City) * {{ill|Sanko Museum|ja|三甲美術館}} (Gifu City) * {{ill|Nawa Insect Museum|ja|名和昆虫博物館}} (Gifu City) * {{ill|Ukai Resource Center|ja|鵜飼資料園}} (Gifu City) ☆ * {{ill|Kitagawa Family Archive|ja|北川家史料館}} (Gifu City) * {{ill|House of Bees|ja|みつばちの家}} (Gifu City) * {{ill|Sōhei Morita Memorial Hall|ja|森田草平記念館}} (Gifu City) * {{ill|Nakakannon-dō and Enkū Resource Center|ja|中観音堂・羽島円空資料館}} ({{ill|Hashima|ja|羽島市}}) * {{ill|Aqua Toto Gifu|ja|アクア・トトぎふ}} ({{ill|Kakamigahara|ja|各務原市}}) * {{ill|Naito Memorial Museum of Medicine|ja|内藤記念くすり博物館}} (Kakamigahara City) * Matsubara Family Nagaya Gate ({{ill|Ginan|ja|岐南町}}) }}
=== [[Seino District]] === {{columns-list|colwidth=25em| * {{ill|Japanese International Poster Museum|ja|日本国際ポスター美術館}} ({{ill|Ōgaki|ja|大垣市}}) * {{ill|Ryōkōji (梁川星巌記念館)|ja|華渓寺}} (Ōgaki City) * {{ill|Gallery Mizuki|ja|ギャラリーみずき}} (Ōgaki City) * {{ill|Shunya Museum|ja|シューニャ美術館}} (Ōgaki City) * {{ill|OKB Gallery Ōgaki|ja|OKBギャラリー}} (Ōgaki City) * {{ill|Rock Candy Museum|ja|氷砂糖資料館}} ({{ill|Kaizu|ja|海津市}}) * {{ill|Myoōji (Sekigahara, Gifu)|ja|妙應寺}} Treasure Hall ({{ill|Sekigahara|ja|関ケ原町}}) ☆ * Katano Memorial Hall ({{ill|Wanouchi, Gifu|ja|輪之内町}}) * Shunka Library ({{ill|Ibigawa, Gifu|ja|揖斐川町}}) }}
(Additional districts and facilities are similarly translated and formatted.)
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/393639.html Machikado Art and History Museums - Gifu Prefecture]

The official name is '''Machikado Art and History Museums'''.
== Overview == The project supports activities to make various cultural assets and artworks owned by individuals and organizations in {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}} widely accessible to the public. It registers museums and galleries capable of collaborating with this initiative as "Machikado Art and History Museums."
The registered museums and galleries vary in scale, ranging from prefectural facilities like the {{ill|Gifu Prefectural Museum|ja|岐阜県博物館}} and the {{ill|Gifu Prefectural Museum of Art|ja|岐阜県美術館}} to privately managed facilities displaying personal collections.
This project began in 1991. The first registered museum was the {{ill|Nakasendō Mini Museum|ja|中山道ミニ博物館}} in {{ill|Tarui, Gifu|ja|垂井町}}, but it was delisted in October 2024<ref name="">{{Cite web|url=http://www.nisimino.com/nisimino/person/25_index_msg.shtml |title=Connecting History to the Present: Director of the Nakasendō Mini Museum|publisher=Nishimino Regional Portal Site |accessdate=2022-05-05}}</ref><ref name="nakasendō">{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/256242.pdf |title=Nakasendō Mini Museum (Machikado Art and History Museums) |format=PDF |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2022-05-05}}</ref>. As of October 16, 2024, there are 97 registered facilities<ref name="gifu">{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/393639.html |title=Machikado Art and History Museums |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2024-11-01}}</ref>.

[[Category:岐阜県まちかど美術館・博物館]][[Category:Machikado Museums]][[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Machikado Museums]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Machikado Museums]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Machikado Museums]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Machikado Museums]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Machikado Museums]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Machikado Museums]][[Category:11]] <!--Machikado Museums--> [[Category:Machikado Museums]][[ja:岐阜県まちかど美術館・博物館]] '''Gifu Prefecture Machikado Art and History Museums''' (岐阜県まちかど美術館・博物館) is a cultural promotion project organized by the Cultural Heritage Division of Gifu Prefecture.
The official name is '''Machikado Art and History Museums'''.
== Overview == The project supports activities to make various cultural assets and artworks owned by individuals and organizations in {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}} widely accessible to the public. It registers museums and galleries capable of collaborating with this initiative as "Machikado Art and History Museums."
The registered museums and galleries vary in scale, ranging from prefectural facilities like the {{ill|Gifu Prefectural Museum|ja|岐阜県博物館}} and the {{ill|Gifu Prefectural Museum of Art|ja|岐阜県美術館}} to privately managed facilities displaying personal collections.
This project began in 1991. The first registered museum was the {{ill|Nakasendō Mini Museum|ja|中山道ミニ博物館}} in {{ill|Tarui, Gifu|ja|垂井町}}, but it was delisted in October 2024<ref name="">{{Cite web|url=http://www.nisimino.com/nisimino/person/25_index_msg.shtml |title=Connecting History to the Present: Director of the Nakasendō Mini Museum|publisher=Nishimino Regional Portal Site |accessdate=2022-05-05}}</ref><ref name="nakasendō">{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/256242.pdf |title=Nakasendō Mini Museum (Machikado Art and History Museums) |format=PDF |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2022-05-05}}</ref>. As of October 16, 2024, there are 97 registered facilities<ref name="gifu">{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/393639.html |title=Machikado Art and History Museums |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2024-11-01}}</ref>.

=== [[Seino District]] === {{columns-list|colwidth=25em| * {{ill|Japanese International Poster Museum|ja|日本国際ポスター美術館}} ({{ill|Ōgaki|ja|大垣市}}) * {{ill|Ryōkōji (梁川星巌記念館)|ja|華渓寺}} (Ōgaki City) * {{ill|Gallery Mizuki|ja|ギャラリーみずき}} (Ōgaki City) * {{ill|Shunya Museum|ja|シューニャ美術館}} (Ōgaki City) * {{ill|OKB Gallery Ōgaki|ja|OKBギャラリー}} (Ōgaki City) * {{ill|Rock Candy Museum|ja|氷砂糖資料館}} ({{ill|Kaizu|ja|海津市}}) * {{ill|Myoōji (Sekigahara, Gifu)|ja|妙應寺}} Treasure Hall ({{ill|Sekigahara|ja|関ケ原町}}) ☆ * Katano Memorial Hall ({{ill|Wanouchi, Gifu|ja|輪之内町}}) * Shunka Library ({{ill|Ibigawa, Gifu|ja|揖斐川町}}) }}

<!--Machikado Museums-->
'''Gifu Prefecture Machikado Art and History Museums''' (岐阜県まちかど美術館・博物館) is a cultural promotion project organized by the Cultural Heritage Division of Gifu Prefecture.
The official name is '''Machikado Art and History Museums'''.
== Overview == The project supports activities to make various cultural assets and artworks owned by individuals and organizations in {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}} widely accessible to the public. It registers museums and galleries capable of collaborating with this initiative as "Machikado Art and History Museums."
The registered museums and galleries vary in scale, ranging from prefectural facilities like the {{ill|Gifu Prefectural Museum|ja|岐阜県博物館}} and the {{ill|Gifu Prefectural Museum of Art|ja|岐阜県美術館}} to privately managed facilities displaying personal collections.
This project began in 1991. The first registered museum was the {{ill|Nakasendō Mini Museum|ja|中山道ミニ博物館}} in {{ill|Tarui, Gifu|ja|垂井町}}, but it was delisted in October 2024<ref name="">{{Cite web|url=http://www.nisimino.com/nisimino/person/25_index_msg.shtml |title=Connecting History to the Present: Director of the Nakasendō Mini Museum|publisher=Nishimino Regional Portal Site |accessdate=2022-05-05}}</ref><ref name="nakasendō">{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/256242.pdf |title=Nakasendō Mini Museum (Machikado Art and History Museums) |format=PDF |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2022-05-05}}</ref>. As of October 16, 2024, there are 97 registered facilities<ref name="gifu">{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/393639.html |title=Machikado Art and History Museums |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2024-11-01}}</ref>.
== List of Museums == ☆ indicates facilities currently closed as of October 2024.
=== [[Gifu District]] === {{columns-list|colwidth=25em| * {{ill|Gifu Prefectural Museum of Art|ja|岐阜県美術館}} ({{ill|Gifu|ja|岐阜市}}) * {{ill|Gifu Prefectural Museum of History|ja|岐阜県歴史資料館}} (Gifu City) * {{ill|Sanko Museum|ja|三甲美術館}} (Gifu City) * {{ill|Nawa Insect Museum|ja|名和昆虫博物館}} (Gifu City) * {{ill|Ukai Resource Center|ja|鵜飼資料園}} (Gifu City) ☆ * {{ill|Kitagawa Family Archive|ja|北川家史料館}} (Gifu City) * {{ill|House of Bees|ja|みつばちの家}} (Gifu City) * {{ill|Sōhei Morita Memorial Hall|ja|森田草平記念館}} (Gifu City) * {{ill|Nakakannon-dō and Enkū Resource Center|ja|中観音堂・羽島円空資料館}} ({{ill|Hashima|ja|羽島市}}) * {{ill|Aqua Toto Gifu|ja|アクア・トトぎふ}} ({{ill|Kakamigahara|ja|各務原市}}) * {{ill|Naito Memorial Museum of Medicine|ja|内藤記念くすり博物館}} (Kakamigahara City) * Matsubara Family Nagaya Gate ({{ill|Ginan|ja|岐南町}}) }}
=== [[Seino District]] === {{columns-list|colwidth=25em| * {{ill|Japanese International Poster Museum|ja|日本国際ポスター美術館}} ({{ill|Ōgaki|ja|大垣市}}) * {{ill|Ryōkōji (梁川星巌記念館)|ja|華渓寺}} (Ōgaki City) * {{ill|Gallery Mizuki|ja|ギャラリーみずき}} (Ōgaki City) * {{ill|Shunya Museum|ja|シューニャ美術館}} (Ōgaki City) * {{ill|OKB Gallery Ōgaki|ja|OKBギャラリー}} (Ōgaki City) * {{ill|Rock Candy Museum|ja|氷砂糖資料館}} ({{ill|Kaizu|ja|海津市}}) * {{ill|Myoōji (Sekigahara, Gifu)|ja|妙應寺}} Treasure Hall ({{ill|Sekigahara|ja|関ケ原町}}) ☆ * Katano Memorial Hall ({{ill|Wanouchi, Gifu|ja|輪之内町}}) * Shunka Library ({{ill|Ibigawa, Gifu|ja|揖斐川町}}) }}
(Additional districts and facilities are similarly translated and formatted.)
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/393639.html Machikado Art and History Museums - Gifu Prefecture]

The registered museums and galleries vary in scale, ranging from prefectural facilities like the {{ill|Gifu Prefectural Museum|ja|岐阜県博物館|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Gifu Prefectural Museum of Art|ja|岐阜県美術館|12=draft}} to privately managed facilities displaying personal collections.
This project began in 1991. The first registered museum was the {{ill|Nakasendō Mini Museum|ja|中山道ミニ博物館|12=draft}} in {{ill|Tarui, Gifu|ja|垂井町|12=draft}}, but it was delisted in October 2024<ref name="">{{Cite web|url=http://www.nisimino.com/nisimino/person/25_index_msg.shtml |title=Connecting History to the Present: Director of the Nakasendō Mini Museum|publisher=Nishimino Regional Portal Site |accessdate=2022-05-05}}</ref><ref name="nakasendō">{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/256242.pdf |title=Nakasendō Mini Museum (Machikado Art and History Museums) |format=PDF |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2022-05-05}}</ref>. As of October 16, 2024, there are 97 registered facilities<ref name="gifu">{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/393639.html |title=Machikado Art and History Museums |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2024-11-01}}</ref>.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:岐阜県まちかど美術館・博物館]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q124774175]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:56, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Gifu Prefecture Machikado Art and History Museums''' (岐阜県まちかど美術館・博物館) is a cultural promotion project organized by the Cultural Heritage Division of Gifu Prefecture.
The official name is '''Machikado Art and History Museums'''.
== Overview == The project supports activities to make various cultural assets and artworks owned by individuals and organizations in [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]] widely accessible to the public. It registers museums and galleries capable of collaborating with this initiative as "Machikado Art and History Museums."
The registered museums and galleries vary in scale, ranging from prefectural facilities like the [[:en:Gifu Prefectural Museum|Gifu Prefectural Museum]] and the [[:en:Museum of Fine Arts, Gifu|Gifu Prefectural Museum of Art]] to privately managed facilities displaying personal collections.
This project began in 1991. The first registered museum was the {{ill|Nakasendō Mini Museum|ja|中山道ミニ博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakasendō Mini Museum|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Tarui, Gifu|Tarui, Gifu]], but it was delisted in October 2024<ref name="">{{Cite web|url=http://www.nisimino.com/nisimino/person/25_index_msg.shtml |title=Connecting History to the Present: Director of the Nakasendō Mini Museum|publisher=Nishimino Regional Portal Site |accessdate=2022-05-05}}</ref><ref name="nakasendō">{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/256242.pdf |title=Nakasendō Mini Museum (Machikado Art and History Museums) |format=PDF |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2022-05-05}}</ref>. As of October 16, 2024, there are 97 registered facilities<ref name="gifu">{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/393639.html |title=Machikado Art and History Museums |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2024-11-01}}</ref>.
== List of Museums == ☆ indicates facilities currently closed as of October 2024.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:岐阜県まちかど美術館・博物館]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q124774175]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:56, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Gifu Prefecture Machikado Art and History Museums''' (岐阜県まちかど美術館・博物館) is a cultural promotion project organized by the Cultural Heritage Division of Gifu Prefecture.
The official name is '''Machikado Art and History Museums'''.
== Overview == The project supports activities to make various cultural assets and artworks owned by individuals and organizations in [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]] widely accessible to the public. It registers museums and galleries capable of collaborating with this initiative as "Machikado Art and History Museums."
The registered museums and galleries vary in scale, ranging from prefectural facilities like the [[:en:Gifu Prefectural Museum|Gifu Prefectural Museum]] and the [[:en:Museum of Fine Arts, Gifu|Gifu Prefectural Museum of Art]] to privately managed facilities displaying personal collections.
This project began in 1991. The first registered museum was the {{ill|Nakasendō Mini Museum|ja|中山道ミニ博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakasendō Mini Museum|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Tarui, Gifu|Tarui, Gifu]], but it was delisted in October 2024<ref name="">{{Cite web|url=http://www.nisimino.com/nisimino/person/25_index_msg.shtml |title=Connecting History to the Present: Director of the Nakasendō Mini Museum|publisher=Nishimino Regional Portal Site |accessdate=2022-05-05}}</ref><ref name="nakasendō">{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/256242.pdf |title=Nakasendō Mini Museum (Machikado Art and History Museums) |format=PDF |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2022-05-05}}</ref>. As of October 16, 2024, there are 97 registered facilities<ref name="gifu">{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/393639.html |title=Machikado Art and History Museums |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2024-11-01}}</ref>.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:岐阜県まちかど美術館・博物館]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q124774175]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:56, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Gifu Prefecture Machikado Art and History Museums''' (岐阜県まちかど美術館・博物館) is a cultural promotion project organized by the Cultural Heritage Division of Gifu Prefecture.
The official name is '''Machikado Art and History Museums'''.
== Overview == The project supports activities to make various cultural assets and artworks owned by individuals and organizations in [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]] widely accessible to the public. It registers museums and galleries capable of collaborating with this initiative as "Machikado Art and History Museums."
The registered museums and galleries vary in scale, ranging from prefectural facilities like the [[:en:Gifu Prefectural Museum|Gifu Prefectural Museum]] and the [[:en:Museum of Fine Arts, Gifu|Gifu Prefectural Museum of Art]] to privately managed facilities displaying personal collections.
This project began in 1991. The first registered museum was the {{ill|Nakasendō Mini Museum|ja|中山道ミニ博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakasendō Mini Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Tarui, Gifu|Tarui, Gifu]], but it was delisted in October 2024<ref name="">{{Cite web|url=http://www.nisimino.com/nisimino/person/25_index_msg.shtml |title=Connecting History to the Present: Director of the Nakasendō Mini Museum|publisher=Nishimino Regional Portal Site |accessdate=2022-05-05}}</ref><ref name="nakasendō">{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/256242.pdf |title=Nakasendō Mini Museum (Machikado Art and History Museums) |format=PDF |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2022-05-05}}</ref>. As of October 16, 2024, there are 97 registered facilities<ref name="gifu">{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/393639.html |title=Machikado Art and History Museums |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2024-11-01}}</ref>.

<!--Machikado Museums--><!--Machikado Museums--><!--Machikado Museums--> [[Category:Museums in Gifu Prefecture]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q124774175}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:岐阜県まちかど美術館・博物館]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q124774175]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:56, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Gifu Prefecture Machikado Art and History Museums''' (岐阜県まちかど美術館・博物館) is a cultural promotion project organized by the Cultural Heritage Division of Gifu Prefecture.
The official name is '''Machikado Art and History Museums'''.
== Overview == The project supports activities to make various cultural assets and artworks owned by individuals and organizations in [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]] widely accessible to the public. It registers museums and galleries capable of collaborating with this initiative as "Machikado Art and History Museums."
The registered museums and galleries vary in scale, ranging from prefectural facilities like the [[:en:Gifu Prefectural Museum|Gifu Prefectural Museum]] and the [[:en:Museum of Fine Arts, Gifu|Gifu Prefectural Museum of Art]] to privately managed facilities displaying personal collections.
This project began in 1991. The first registered museum was the {{ill|Nakasendō Mini Museum|ja|中山道ミニ博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakasendō Mini Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Tarui, Gifu|Tarui, Gifu]], but it was delisted in October 2024<ref name="">{{Cite web|url=http://www.nisimino.com/nisimino/person/25_index_msg.shtml |title=Connecting History to the Present: Director of the Nakasendō Mini Museum|publisher=Nishimino Regional Portal Site |accessdate=2022-05-05}}</ref><ref name="nakasendō">{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/256242.pdf |title=Nakasendō Mini Museum (Machikado Art and History Museums) |format=PDF |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2022-05-05}}</ref>. As of October 16, 2024, there are 97 registered facilities<ref name="gifu">{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/393639.html |title=Machikado Art and History Museums |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2024-11-01}}</ref>.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:岐阜県まちかど美術館・博物館]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q124774175]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:56, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Gifu Prefecture Machikado Art and History Museums''' (岐阜県まちかど美術館・博物館) is a cultural promotion project organized by the Cultural Heritage Division of Gifu Prefecture.
The official name is '''Machikado Art and History Museums'''.
== Overview == The project supports activities to make various cultural assets and artworks owned by individuals and organizations in [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]] widely accessible to the public. It registers museums and galleries capable of collaborating with this initiative as "Machikado Art and History Museums."
The registered museums and galleries vary in scale, ranging from prefectural facilities like the [[:en:Gifu Prefectural Museum|Gifu Prefectural Museum]] and the [[:en:Museum of Fine Arts, Gifu|Gifu Prefectural Museum of Art]] to privately managed facilities displaying personal collections.
This project began in 1991. The first registered museum was the {{ill|Nakasendō Mini Museum|ja|中山道ミニ博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakasendō Mini Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Tarui, Gifu|Tarui, Gifu]], but it was delisted in October 2024<ref name="">{{Cite web|url=http://www.nisimino.com/nisimino/person/25_index_msg.shtml |title=Connecting History to the Present: Director of the Nakasendō Mini Museum|publisher=Nishimino Regional Portal Site |accessdate=2022-05-05}}</ref><ref name="nakasendō">{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/256242.pdf |title=Nakasendō Mini Museum (Machikado Art and History Museums) |format=PDF |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2022-05-05}}</ref>. As of October 16, 2024, there are 97 registered facilities<ref name="gifu">{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/393639.html |title=Machikado Art and History Museums |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2024-11-01}}</ref>.
== List of Museums == ☆ indicates facilities currently closed as of October 2024.

=== [[Seino District]] === {{columns-list|colwidth=25em| * {{ill|Japanese International Poster Museum|ja|日本国際ポスター美術館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese International Poster Museum|1=Japanese International Poster Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q111803091}} ([[:en:Ōgaki|Ōgaki]]) * {{ill|Ryōkōji (梁川星巌記念館)|ja|華渓寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryōkōji (梁川星巌記念館)|1=Kakei-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q54152993}} (Ōgaki City) * {{ill|Gallery Mizuki|ja|ギャラリーみずき|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gallery Mizuki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ōgaki City) * {{ill|Shunya Museum|ja|シューニャ美術館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shunya Museum|1=Shunya Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11308090}} (Ōgaki City) * {{ill|OKB Gallery Ōgaki|ja|OKBギャラリー|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/OKB Gallery Ōgaki|1=OKB Gallery Ōgaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134405381}} (Ōgaki City) * {{ill|Rock Candy Museum|ja|氷砂糖資料館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rock Candy Museum|1=Rock Candy Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549632}} ([[:en:Kaizu|Kaizu]]) * {{ill|Myoōji (Sekigahara, Gifu)|ja|妙應寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoōji (Sekigahara, Gifu)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 妙応寺|1=Myoōji (Sekigahara, Gifu)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30926149}} Treasure Hall ([[:en:Sekigahara, Gifu|Sekigahara]]) ☆ * Katano Memorial Hall ([[:en:Wanouchi, Gifu|Wanouchi, Gifu]]) * Shunka Library ([[:en:Ibigawa|Ibigawa, Gifu]]) }}

== Overview == The project supports activities to make various cultural assets and artworks owned by individuals and organizations in [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]] widely accessible to the public. The registered museums and galleries vary in scale, ranging from prefectural facilities like the [[:en:Gifu Prefectural Museum|Gifu Prefectural Museum]] and the [[:en:Museum of Fine Arts, Gifu|Gifu Prefectural Museum of Art]] to privately managed facilities displaying personal collections.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:岐阜県まちかど美術館・博物館]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:岐阜県まちかど美術館・博物館]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:岐阜県まちかど美術館・博物館]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q124774175]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:56, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Gifu Prefecture Machikado Art and History Museums''' (岐阜県まちかど美術館・博物館) is a cultural promotion project organized by the Cultural Heritage Division of Gifu Prefecture.
The official name is '''Machikado Art and History Museums'''.
== Overview == The project supports activities to make various cultural assets and artworks owned by individuals and organizations in [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]] widely accessible to the public. It registers museums and galleries capable of collaborating with this initiative as "Machikado Art and History Museums."
The registered museums and galleries vary in scale, ranging from prefectural facilities like the [[:en:Gifu Prefectural Museum|Gifu Prefectural Museum]] and the [[:en:Museum of Fine Arts, Gifu|Gifu Prefectural Museum of Art]] to privately managed facilities displaying personal collections.
This project began in 1991. The first registered museum was the {{ill|Nakasendō Mini Museum|ja|中山道ミニ博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakasendō Mini Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Tarui, Gifu|Tarui, Gifu]], but it was delisted in October 2024<ref name="">{{Cite web|url=http://www.nisimino.com/nisimino/person/25_index_msg.shtml |title=Connecting History to the Present: Director of the Nakasendō Mini Museum|publisher=Nishimino Regional Portal Site |accessdate=2022-05-05}}</ref><ref name="nakasendō">{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/256242.pdf |title=Nakasendō Mini Museum (Machikado Art and History Museums) |format=PDF |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2022-05-05}}</ref>. As of October 16, 2024, there are 97 registered facilities<ref name="gifu">{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/393639.html |title=Machikado Art and History Museums |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2024-11-01}}</ref>.

== Overview == The project supports activities to make various cultural assets and artworks owned by individuals and organizations in [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]] widely accessible to the public. The registered museums and galleries vary in scale, ranging from prefectural facilities like the [[:en:Gifu Prefectural Museum|Gifu Prefectural Museum]] and the [[:en:Museum of Fine Arts, Gifu|Gifu Prefectural Museum of Art]] to privately managed facilities displaying personal collections.

revision.comment: v25: Convert interwiki links [[:en:...]] to {{ill}} templates
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:岐阜県まちかど美術館・博物館]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:岐阜県まちかど美術館・博物館]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:岐阜県まちかど美術館・博物館]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q124774175]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:56, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Gifu Prefecture Machikado Art and History Museums''' (岐阜県まちかど美術館・博物館) is a cultural promotion project organized by the Cultural Heritage Division of Gifu Prefecture.
The official name is '''Machikado Art and History Museums'''.
== Overview == The project supports activities to make various cultural assets and artworks owned by individuals and organizations in {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture}} widely accessible to the public. It registers museums and galleries capable of collaborating with this initiative as "Machikado Art and History Museums."
The registered museums and galleries vary in scale, ranging from prefectural facilities like the {{ill|Gifu Prefectural Museum|en|Gifu Prefectural Museum|WD=Q11471444|lt=Gifu Prefectural Museum}} and the {{ill|Museum of Fine Arts, Gifu|en|Museum of Fine Arts, Gifu|WD=Q5359197|lt=Gifu Prefectural Museum of Art}} to privately managed facilities displaying personal collections.
This project began in 1991. The first registered museum was the {{ill|Nakasendō Mini Museum|ja|中山道ミニ博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakasendō Mini Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Tarui, Gifu|en|Tarui, Gifu|WD=Q1345531|lt=Tarui, Gifu}}, but it was delisted in October 2024<ref name="">{{Cite web|url=http://www.nisimino.com/nisimino/person/25_index_msg.shtml |title=Connecting History to the Present: Director of the Nakasendō Mini Museum|publisher=Nishimino Regional Portal Site |accessdate=2022-05-05}}</ref><ref name="nakasendō">{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/256242.pdf |title=Nakasendō Mini Museum (Machikado Art and History Museums) |format=PDF |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2022-05-05}}</ref>. As of October 16, 2024, there are 97 registered facilities<ref name="gifu">{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/393639.html |title=Machikado Art and History Museums |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2024-11-01}}</ref>.
== List of Museums == ☆ indicates facilities currently closed as of October 2024.

=== [[Seino District]] === {{columns-list|colwidth=25em| * {{ill|Japanese International Poster Museum|ja|日本国際ポスター美術館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese International Poster Museum|1=Japanese International Poster Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q111803091}} ({{ill|Ōgaki|en|Ōgaki|WD=Q384926|lt=Ōgaki}}) * {{ill|Ryōkōji (梁川星巌記念館)|ja|華渓寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryōkōji (梁川星巌記念館)|1=Kakei-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q54152993}} (Ōgaki City) * {{ill|Gallery Mizuki|ja|ギャラリーみずき|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gallery Mizuki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ōgaki City) * {{ill|Shunya Museum|ja|シューニャ美術館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shunya Museum|1=Shunya Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11308090}} (Ōgaki City) * {{ill|OKB Gallery Ōgaki|ja|OKBギャラリー|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/OKB Gallery Ōgaki|1=OKB Gallery Ōgaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134405381}} (Ōgaki City) * {{ill|Rock Candy Museum|ja|氷砂糖資料館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rock Candy Museum|1=Rock Candy Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549632}} ({{ill|Kaizu|en|Kaizu|WD=Q844056|lt=Kaizu}}) * {{ill|Myoōji (Sekigahara, Gifu)|ja|妙應寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoōji (Sekigahara, Gifu)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 妙応寺|1=Myoōji (Sekigahara, Gifu)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30926149}} Treasure Hall ({{ill|Sekigahara, Gifu|en|Sekigahara, Gifu|WD=Q427600|lt=Sekigahara}}) ☆ * Katano Memorial Hall ({{ill|Wanouchi, Gifu|en|Wanouchi, Gifu|WD=Q1204291|lt=Wanouchi, Gifu}}) * Shunka Library ({{ill|Ibigawa|en|Ibigawa|WD=Q1204046|lt=Ibigawa, Gifu}}) }}

The official name is '''Machikado Art and History Museums'''.

It registers museums and galleries capable of collaborating with this initiative as "Machikado Art and History Museums."
The registered museums and galleries vary in scale, ranging from prefectural facilities like the {{ill|Gifu Prefectural Museum|en|Gifu Prefectural Museum|WD=Q11471444|lt=Gifu Prefectural Museum}} and the {{ill|Museum of Fine Arts, Gifu|en|Museum of Fine Arts, Gifu|WD=Q5359197|lt=Gifu Prefectural Museum of Art}} to privately managed facilities displaying personal collections.
This project began in 1991. The first registered museum was the {{ill|Nakasendō Mini Museum|ja|中山道ミニ博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakasendō Mini Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Tarui, Gifu|en|Tarui, Gifu|WD=Q1345531|lt=Tarui, Gifu}}, but it was delisted in October 2024<ref name="">{{Cite web|url=http://www.nisimino.com/nisimino/person/25_index_msg.shtml |title=Connecting History to the Present: Director of the Nakasendō Mini Museum|publisher=Nishimino Regional Portal Site |accessdate=2022-05-05}}</ref><ref name="nakasendō">{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/256242.pdf |title=Nakasendō Mini Museum (Machikado Art and History Museums) |format=PDF |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2022-05-05}}</ref>. As of October 16, 2024, there are 97 registered facilities<ref name="gifu">{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/393639.html |title=Machikado Art and History Museums |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2024-11-01}}</ref>.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:岐阜県まちかど美術館・博物館]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:岐阜県まちかど美術館・博物館]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:岐阜県まちかど美術館・博物館]]
'''Gifu Prefecture Machikado Art and History Museums''' (岐阜県まちかど美術館・博物館) is a cultural promotion project organized by the Cultural Heritage Division of Gifu Prefecture.
The official name is '''Machikado Art and History Museums'''.
== Overview == The project supports activities to make various cultural assets and artworks owned by individuals and organizations in {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture}} widely accessible to the public. It registers museums and galleries capable of collaborating with this initiative as "Machikado Art and History Museums."
The registered museums and galleries vary in scale, ranging from prefectural facilities like the {{ill|Gifu Prefectural Museum|en|Gifu Prefectural Museum|WD=Q11471444|lt=Gifu Prefectural Museum}} and the {{ill|Museum of Fine Arts, Gifu|en|Museum of Fine Arts, Gifu|WD=Q5359197|lt=Gifu Prefectural Museum of Art}} to privately managed facilities displaying personal collections.
This project began in 1991. The first registered museum was the {{ill|Nakasendō Mini Museum|ja|中山道ミニ博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakasendō Mini Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Tarui, Gifu|en|Tarui, Gifu|WD=Q1345531|lt=Tarui, Gifu}}, but it was delisted in October 2024<ref name="">{{Cite web|url=http://www.nisimino.com/nisimino/person/25_index_msg.shtml |title=Connecting History to the Present: Director of the Nakasendō Mini Museum|publisher=Nishimino Regional Portal Site |accessdate=2022-05-05}}</ref><ref name="nakasendō">{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/256242.pdf |title=Nakasendō Mini Museum (Machikado Art and History Museums) |format=PDF |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2022-05-05}}</ref>. As of October 16, 2024, there are 97 registered facilities<ref name="gifu">{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/393639.html |title=Machikado Art and History Museums |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2024-11-01}}</ref>.
== List of Museums == ☆ indicates facilities currently closed as of October 2024.

'''Gifu Prefecture Machikado Art and History Museums''' (岐阜県まちかど美術館・博物館) is a cultural promotion project organized by the Cultural Heritage Division of Gifu Prefecture.
The official name is '''Machikado Art and History Museums'''.
== Overview == The project supports activities to make various cultural assets and artworks owned by individuals and organizations in {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}} widely accessible to the public. It registers museums and galleries capable of collaborating with this initiative as "Machikado Art and History Museums."
The registered museums and galleries vary in scale, ranging from prefectural facilities like the {{ill|Gifu Prefectural Museum|en|Gifu Prefectural Museum|WD=Q11471444|lt=Gifu Prefectural Museum|1=Gifu Prefectural Museum}} and the {{ill|Museum of Fine Arts, Gifu|en|Museum of Fine Arts, Gifu|WD=Q5359197|lt=Gifu Prefectural Museum of Art|1=Museum of Fine Arts, Gifu}} to privately managed facilities displaying personal collections.
This project began in 1991. The first registered museum was the {{ill|Nakasendō Mini Museum|ja|中山道ミニ博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakasendō Mini Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Tarui, Gifu|en|Tarui, Gifu|WD=Q1345531|lt=Tarui, Gifu|1=Tarui, Gifu}}, but it was delisted in October 2024<ref name="">{{Cite web|url=http://www.nisimino.com/nisimino/person/25_index_msg.shtml |title=Connecting History to the Present: Director of the Nakasendō Mini Museum|publisher=Nishimino Regional Portal Site |accessdate=2022-05-05}}</ref><ref name="nakasendō">{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/256242.pdf |title=Nakasendō Mini Museum (Machikado Art and History Museums) |format=PDF |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2022-05-05}}</ref>. As of October 16, 2024, there are 97 registered facilities<ref name="gifu">{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/393639.html |title=Machikado Art and History Museums |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2024-11-01}}</ref>.

'''Gifu Prefecture Machikado Art and History Museums''' (岐阜県まちかど美術館・博物館) is a cultural promotion project organized by the Cultural Heritage Division of Gifu Prefecture.
The official name is '''Machikado Art and History Museums'''.
== Overview == The project supports activities to make various cultural assets and artworks owned by individuals and organizations in {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|qid=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}} widely accessible to the public. It registers museums and galleries capable of collaborating with this initiative as "Machikado Art and History Museums."
The registered museums and galleries vary in scale, ranging from prefectural facilities like the {{ill|Gifu Prefectural Museum|en|Gifu Prefectural Museum|qid=Q11471444|lt=Gifu Prefectural Museum|1=Gifu Prefectural Museum}} and the {{ill|Museum of Fine Arts, Gifu|en|Museum of Fine Arts, Gifu|qid=Q5359197|lt=Gifu Prefectural Museum of Art|1=Museum of Fine Arts, Gifu}} to privately managed facilities displaying personal collections.
This project began in 1991. The first registered museum was the {{ill|Nakasendō Mini Museum|ja|中山道ミニ博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakasendō Mini Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Tarui, Gifu|en|Tarui, Gifu|qid=Q1345531|lt=Tarui, Gifu|1=Tarui, Gifu}}, but it was delisted in October 2024<ref name="">{{Cite web|url=http://www.nisimino.com/nisimino/person/25_index_msg.shtml |title=Connecting History to the Present: Director of the Nakasendō Mini Museum|publisher=Nishimino Regional Portal Site |accessdate=2022-05-05}}</ref><ref name="nakasendō">{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/256242.pdf |title=Nakasendō Mini Museum (Machikado Art and History Museums) |format=PDF |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2022-05-05}}</ref>. As of October 16, 2024, there are 97 registered facilities<ref name="gifu">{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/393639.html |title=Machikado Art and History Museums |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2024-11-01}}</ref>.
